examples.title: First Fit Memory Allocation
examples.description: Algorithm to allocate memory using First Fit strategy
examples.code:
STRUCTURE MemoryBlock
  START_ADDRESS, SIZE, PROCESS_ID, IS_FREE
END_STRUCTURE

FUNCTION FIRST_FIT(memory_blocks, request_size)
  FOR EACH block IN memory_blocks
    IF block.IS_FREE AND block.SIZE >= request_size
      IF block.SIZE > request_size
        SPLIT_BLOCK(block, request_size)
      END_IF
      block.IS_FREE = FALSE
      block.PROCESS_ID = current_process
      RETURN block.START_ADDRESS
    END_IF
  END_FOR
  RETURN -1  // No suitable block found
END_FUNCTION
examples.explanation: First Fit searches memory from the beginning and allocates the first hole that is large enough. It may result in fast allocation but can leave large unused holes near the beginning.

examples.title: First Fit Implementation in Python
examples.description: Implementation of First Fit memory allocation algorithm
examples.code:
class MemoryBlock:
    def __init__(self, start, size, process_id=None, free=True):
        self.start = start
        self.size = size
        self.process_id = process_id
        self.free = free

def first_fit(blocks, request_size, process_id):
    """Allocate memory using First Fit algorithm"""
    for block in blocks:
        if block.free and block.size >= request_size:
            if block.size > request_size:
                # Split the block
                new_block = MemoryBlock(
                    block.start + request_size,
                    block.size - request_size
                )
                block.size = request_size
                blocks.insert(blocks.index(block) + 1, new_block)
            
            block.free = False
            block.process_id = process_id
            return block.start
    return -1  # No suitable block found

# Example usage
memory = [
    MemoryBlock(0, 200),
    MemoryBlock(200, 100),
    MemoryBlock(300, 150),
    MemoryBlock(450, 250)
]

# Allocate 80KB to process P1
addr = first_fit(memory, 80, "P1")
print(f"Allocated at address: {addr}")
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates First Fit allocation. It iterates through memory blocks in order and allocates the first block large enough. If the block is larger than requested, it splits the block.

examples.title: Best Fit Allocation in C
examples.description: Implementation of Best Fit memory allocation algorithm
examples.explanation: This C implementation finds the smallest free block that can accommodate the request, minimizing wasted space. Best Fit produces the smallest leftover hole but requires searching all blocks.

examples.title: Address Translation Simulation
examples.description: Simulates address translation using base and limit registers
examples.explanation: This Java code simulates the Memory Management Unit functionality. The base register stores the starting physical address, and the limit register stores the process size. Every logical address is checked against the limit and then translated by adding the base.
